creator: markir@paradise.net.nz
text:
Xorg 7.3 appears to work ok - starts and shuts down, but I see this in the log

Fatal server error:
Caught signal 11.  Server aborting

and there is an Xorg.core left. Tracing it gives:

(gdb) bt
#0  0x485d19e7 in kill () from /lib/libc.so.7
#1  0x485d1946 in raise () from /lib/libc.so.7
#2  0x485d054a in abort () from /lib/libc.so.7
#3  0x080b8123 in ddxGiveUp ()
#4  0x080b81f7 in AbortDDX ()
#5  0x08225708 in AbortServer ()
#6  0x08225aa9 in FatalError ()
#7  0x080de028 in xf86SigHandler ()
#8  0xbfbfffb4 in ?? ()
#9  0x0000000b in ?? ()
#10 0x00000001 in ?? ()
#11 0xbfbfe940 in ?? ()
#12 0x2827127c in ?? ()
#13 0x080ddf70 in xf86InterceptSigIll ()
#14 0x080b56c5 in config_fini ()
#15 0x080b5a6b in config_dbus_core_fini ()
#16 0x080b55d0 in config_fini ()
#17 0x0806dc36 in main ()

How-To-Repeat: Start and run X for a few seconds or so, and then shut it down.

The hardware I'm running on is Supermicro P3DER, whose video bits FreeBSD detects as:

drm0: <Rage XL AGP 2X> on vgapci0
